profiles.description: User profiles extending Supabase auth.users with onboarding, tier, and session tracking

email_verified.description: Set true via Supabase Auth webhook on email confirm

tier.description: Always 'free' at launch; Pro unlocked via M15 later

triggers: on_auth_user_created → auto-create profile row with email from auth.users, onboarding_step='username', tier='free'
on_email_confirmed (Supabase Auth webhook) → set email_verified = true

categories.description: Bookmark categories with one-level nesting support

bookmarks.description: Core bookmarks table with full-text search
triggers: on_insert_update → rebuild search_vec from title + description + tags + url
on_update(category_id) → adjust bookmark_count on old and new category

rls.select_public: is_active = true (anon access for portable view)

M1.name: Auth & Identity
M1.scope: Registration, login, email verification, session management, onboarding wizard, profile setup, tier enforcement groundwork

components.name: login-form
features: Error handling (invalid email, rate limit, network)
'Check your email' confirmation screen after magic link sent

components.name: signup-form
features: Terms of service + privacy policy checkbox
Redirects to verify-email after email signup
Redirects to onboarding after OAuth signup
Error handling (email already registered, rate limit)

components.name: verify-email
features: Pending verification screen with email address displayed
Resend verification email button with 60-second cooldown timer
Auto-redirect to onboarding once verified (polls or listens via Supabase Realtime)
'Wrong email?' link to go back to signup
Skipped entirely for OAuth users (already verified by provider)

components.name: onboarding-wizard
features: Step 1 — Choose Username: input with live availability check (debounced), slug preview showing 'stacked.app/yourname', optional display name, optional avatar upload

Step 2 — Pick Starter Categories: grid of preset category cards (Dev, Design, News, Reading, Tools, Social, Shopping, Recipes, etc.), multi-select with visual feedback, minimum 1 required, creates selected categories in DB
Step 3 — Add First Bookmark: URL input with auto-metadata fetch, metadata preview (favicon + title + description), category dropdown (from Step 2 picks), skip option ('I'll add later')
Skip option per step (except username which is required)
'Get Started' CTA on final step → sets onboarding_step='done' → redirects to dashboard
Each step persists to DB independently (safe to close and resume)

components.name: auth-callback
features: Check if profile exists → if new user, redirect to onboarding
Check onboarding_step → if not 'done', redirect to onboarding
If returning user with completed onboarding → redirect to dashboard

components.name: auth-guard
features: Layout wrapper component for protected routes
Checks Supabase session → redirects to /login if unauthenticated
Checks profile.onboarding_step → redirects to /onboarding if not 'done'
Renders loading skeleton during session check
Provides user context to child components via React Context

api_routes.description: Get current user profile including tier and onboarding status
Submit onboarding step data (username, categories, first bookmark)

edge_cases: Username uniqueness validation (race condition: check + create must be atomic)
OAuth account linking (same email, different provider → Supabase handles via email matching)
Session expiry handling mid-action (show re-login modal, not hard redirect)
Redirect to original page after login (store intended URL in sessionStorage)
User closes browser during onboarding → resume at last completed step on next login
Email verification link expired → show 'link expired' page with resend button
OAuth user skips email verification entirely (provider already verified)
Username taken between availability check and submission → graceful error with suggestions

tier_enforcement.description: Tier limits enforced at API layer from day one, even though only 'free' tier exists at launch. This ensures Pro tier activation (M15) requires zero API changes.
implementation: Every create/write API route calls checkTierLimit(userId, resource) before proceeding
checkTierLimit reads profiles.tier and compares against TIER_LIMITS constant
On limit hit: return 403 with { code: 'TIER_LIMIT_REACHED', limit: 500, current: 500, resource: 'bookmarks' }
Frontend shows upgrade prompt (at launch: 'limit reached' message; post-M15: upgrade CTA)

M2.name: Category Engine
M2.scope: Full CRUD for categories, nesting, ordering, visual customization

components.name: category-tree
features: Drop bookmark onto category to move

components.name: category-card
features: Large card with name, color, icon, count

components.name: category-badge
features: Inline pill with category color background

business_rules: Every user gets a 'General' default category on signup
A category cannot be its own parent
Max nesting depth of 1 (parent → child only)
On delete: prompt user to move bookmarks or delete them
Default category cannot be deleted

M3.name: Bookmark Core
M3.scope: Full CRUD for bookmarks, metadata display, tagging, pinning, bulk actions

components.name: bookmark-card
features: Hover actions: edit, delete, open in new tab, copy URL

components.name: bookmark-stack
features: USP: Visual card pile grouped by category
Shows top card with category name and color
Hover to peek (slight offset reveal via Framer Motion)
Click to fan-out with spring physics animation
Empty state for categories with no bookmarks

components.name: bookmark-grid
features: CSS Grid with auto-fill, min 280px columns
Masonry option for varied card heights

components.name: bookmark-form
features: URL input field with paste detection
Auto-fetch trigger on valid URL (calls M4)
Manual override for all auto-filled fields

components.name: bookmark-actions
features: Actions: Move to category, Delete, Archive, Add tags, Pin/Unpin

components.name: bookmark-skeleton
features: Card variant, list-item variant, stack variant

api_routes.description: Bulk action (move, delete, archive, tag, pin)

business_rules: URL is required; title auto-filled from metadata
Duplicate URL detection: warn user, don't block
Pinned bookmarks always sort first within their category
Tags stored as PostgreSQL TEXT[] array
updated_at auto-set via trigger on row update
Domain extracted from URL on insert (trigger)
Archived bookmarks hidden from default view

M4.name: Metadata Fetcher
M4.scope: Server-side URL scraping for title, description, favicon, and OG image

edge_function.name: fetch-metadata

output.title: string | null
output.favicon_url: string | null (Supabase Storage URL after caching)
output.og_image_url: string | null (Supabase Storage URL after caching)

behavior: Fetch target URL HTML with 8s timeout
Download favicon and OG image to Supabase Storage
Generate fallback favicon (domain initial + color) if none found
Handle non-HTML responses gracefully (return domain only)
Rate limit: 30 requests per user per minute

business_rules: Timeout after 8 seconds
Follow up to 3 redirects
If fetch fails, return empty metadata (user fills manually)
Fallback favicon: colored circle with domain first letter
Skip fetch for known non-HTML domains (e.g., direct file links)

M5.name: Search & Discovery
M5.scope: Full-text search, filtering, sorting, and discovery of bookmarks

components.name: search-bar
features: Live result count as you type

components.name: search-results
features: Results list using bookmark-card or bookmark-list-item

api_routes.description: Full-text search with filters and pagination

implementation_notes: PostgreSQL trigger rebuilds search_vec on insert/update
Uses to_tsvector('english', title || ' ' || coalesce(description,'') || ' ' || array_to_string(tags,' ') || ' ' || url)
Query uses plainto_tsquery with ts_rank for relevance
GIN index on search_vec for performance

M6.name: Import / Export
M6.scope: Import bookmarks from browsers, export in standard formats

components.name: import-wizard
features: Step 2: File upload with drag-and-drop zone
Step 3: Preview parsed data + duplicate strategy

components.name: import-preview
features: Table of parsed bookmarks: URL, title, detected folder
Duplicate strategy selector (skip / overwrite / create new)
Toggle items on/off for selective import

components.name: export-options
features: Category filter: export all or selected categories

api_routes.description: Parse file and return preview (dry run)

business_rules: HTML import parses Netscape Bookmark Format (universal browser export)
Folders in HTML map to categories (auto-create if not existing)
Large imports (1000+) processed in batches of 100
Max import file size: 10MB
JSON export is re-importable into Stacked
CSV export includes: url, title, description, category, tags, created_at

M7.name: Portable Public View
M7.scope: Shareable, read-only bookmark pages with theme customization

components.name: portable-theme
features: Applies selected theme: minimal, cards, masonry, terminal
Dark mode support (respects visitor's system preference)
Custom font per theme (e.g., terminal uses monospace)

pages.description: Public portable view. Fetches shared_link by slug, loads bookmarks and categories, renders with chosen theme/layout.
Dynamic OG image for social sharing. Shows user name, bookmark count, and theme preview.

security: RLS only exposes data where shared_links.is_active = true
Rate limit on view_count increment to prevent abuse

M8.name: Dashboard Shell
M8.scope: Layout, navigation, and UI framework for authenticated experience

components.name: sidebar
features: Responsive: drawer on mobile, fixed on desktop

components.name: mobile-nav
features: 5 items: Home, Search, Add (center, prominent), Categories, Settings

components.name: theme-toggle
features: Icon changes based on active theme

M9.name: Storage & Asset Management
M9.scope: Persistent storage for favicons, OG images, and user avatars. Favicons and OG images are stored domain-level (dedup) — not per user — to prevent ~30x storage inflation. Avatars remain user-scoped.

storage_paths.domain_hash_spec: first 16 hex chars of SHA-256(normalize(domain)), where normalize = lowercase + strip www.

components.name: favicon
features: Receives faviconUrl prop (null = show fallback immediately)
On image load error, falls back to domain initial on colored square
Color is deterministic from domain string using CATEGORY_COLORS
No fetch logic — purely display from passed-in URL

implementation_notes: domain_assets table is the source of truth for fetch status — M4 writes it, M9 creates it
checkDomainAssetStatus() queries domain_assets table, not a storage HEAD request (faster)
maybe_orphan_domain() SQL function is created here; wired to bookmarks delete trigger in M3
Orphan cleanup: 7-day grace period → cleanup edge function (M4 scope) + weekly cron
Favicons: 90-day TTL. OG images: 30-day TTL (can change more often)
Service role client required to write favicons/og-images (no user-facing RLS write policy)

M10.name: Link Health Monitor
M10.scope: Detect broken, redirected, and timed-out bookmark URLs

components.name: link-status-badge
features: Tooltip: status label + last checked date
Status colors: alive=green, dead=red, redirected=yellow, timeout=gray

edge_function.name: check-links
behavior: Select bookmarks where last_checked_at is NULL or > 7 days ago
Process in batches of 50 with 1s delay between batches
For each URL: send HEAD request with 10s timeout

business_rules: Check interval: weekly per bookmark
HEAD request only (no body download)
Respect robots.txt where possible
Dead links surfaced in dashboard via 'Broken Links' filter

M11.name: Browser Extension
M11.scope: One-click bookmark saving from any webpage

components.name: extension-popup
features: Auto-filled URL and title from active tab
Quick-save mode (save with defaults, no form)

M12.name: Command Palette
M12.scope: Keyboard-first power-user interface for navigation and actions

components.name: command-palette
features: Action commands: 'Add bookmark', 'Go to category', 'Toggle view', 'Open settings'
Fuzzy matching on all searchable content

M13.name: Analytics & Insights
M13.scope: Personal bookmark usage statistics and visualizations

components.name: stats-cards
features: Cards: Total Bookmarks, Added This Week, Total Categories, Broken Links
Each card: icon + count + delta indicator (up/down arrow with percentage)

components.name: growth-chart
features: Bookmarks added per week over last 12 weeks
Tooltip with exact count per week

components.name: category-breakdown
features: Horizontal bar chart or donut chart

components.name: tag-cloud
features: Click to filter bookmarks by tag

implementation_notes: All queries are PostgreSQL aggregations — no separate analytics DB
Growth data grouped by date_trunc('week', created_at)

M14.name: Settings & Preferences
M14.scope: User configuration, account management, and app preferences. Core settings (profile, preferences, danger zone) are MVP. Shared-links management is deferred until M7 (Portable Public View) is built, since it depends on the shared_links table that M7 creates.

components.name: settings-shared-links
components.note: Requires shared_links table created by M7. Build the route shell in MVP with a 'Coming soon' placeholder; activate when M7 is complete.

components.name: settings-danger-zone
features: Cascade delete: all bookmarks, categories, shared links, storage

M15.name: Billing & Tiers
M15.scope: Stripe integration, Pro plan subscription, usage metering, upgrade/downgrade flows, invoicing

subscriptions.description: Stripe subscription records linked to user profiles

planned_components.name: upgrade-prompt
planned_components.description: Modal shown when user hits a tier limit. Shows current usage vs limit, Pro benefits, and CTA to upgrade.

planned_components.name: pricing-page
planned_components.description: Public pricing comparison (Free vs Pro) with feature matrix and Stripe Checkout CTA.

planned_components.name: billing-settings
planned_components.description: Settings page showing current plan, usage meters, invoice history, cancel/downgrade option, Stripe Customer Portal link.

planned_api_routes.description: Create Stripe Checkout session for Pro upgrade

planned_tech.integration: Stripe webhook updates profiles.tier and subscriptions table

notes: Tier enforcement already built into M1 API layer — M15 only needs to flip profiles.tier from 'free' to 'pro'
Stripe webhook is the single source of truth for subscription status
Downgrade: on subscription cancel, tier reverts to 'free' at period end; data is NOT deleted but limits re-apply
Usage metering: bookmark/category/shared_link counts checked against TIER_LIMITS on every create
GDPR data export can be bundled into this module as a settings feature

stack_view.name: Stack View
stack_view.description: Visual card pile metaphor for bookmark collections. Hover to peek, click to fan out.
stack_view.differentiator: No bookmark manager uses a spatial/tactile card stack metaphor. Memorable and brandable.

portable_pages.name: Portable Pages
portable_pages.description: Customizable public start pages at stacked.app/username with theme selection.
portable_pages.differentiator: Linktree for curated knowledge. Developers share toolkits, students share resources.
portable_pages.monetization: Free: 1 page. Pro: unlimited pages + custom themes + custom domains.

smart_collections.name: Smart Collections
smart_collections.description: Rule-based auto-categorization that suggests groupings based on domain and content patterns.
smart_collections.differentiator: Reduces the biggest pain point: manual organizing. Makes the product feel intelligent.

deliverables: Onboarding wizard (3-step: username → categories → first bookmark)
Tier enforcement groundwork (free limits enforced at API layer)
Import from browser HTML / JSON / CSV
Billing settings page with invoice history
Upgrade prompt on tier limit hit

build_order._comment: Canonical implementation sequence derived from module dependency graph. Always build a module after all its dependencies are complete. Status: done | next | pending

sequence.name: Settings & Preferences
sequence.note: Core settings (profile, preferences, danger zone) only. settings-shared-links page is a placeholder until M7.

sequence.name: Portable Public View
sequence.note: After M7 is complete, activate settings-shared-links in M14.

rules: Never start a module before all its deps are marked done
M9 has no deps — can be built in parallel with M2 if needed
M14 core (profile/preferences/danger-zone) needs only M1; build it at step 8 after M8 for logical UX grouping
settings-shared-links (M14) is a deferred component — scaffold as placeholder in MVP, activate after M7
M6 and M7 are interchangeable in order (both depend on M3); do M6 first since it uses existing tables
Update this build_order.sequence[n].status to 'done' as each module ships

server_only.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: Supabase service role key (NEVER expose to client)
server_only.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: Stripe webhook signing secret (Phase 4 — M15 only)
